sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after MOUSE(row=15, col=33). Looking at the image, it seems one of the green blocks disappeared - the one at the middle row that I clicked on (rows 13-17, cols 31-35). Let me verify this by checking the segmentation to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - count green blocks now
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Green (N) blocks now:")
green_nodes = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        green_nodes.append(n['id'])
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print(f"\nTotal green blocks: {len(green_nodes)} (was 7)")

# Check player position
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'Y') and n['pixels'] <= 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Player {n['color']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
